title: Fine-Tuning LLMs Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on LoRA, QLoRA, Quantization, and PEFT.

questions.question: What is the primary business and technical purpose of 'Fine-Tuning' an open-source Large Language Model (LLM)?

options.text: To compress the model so it can be emailed as an attachment.

To leverage a model's vast preexisting general intelligence by retraining it slightly on a localized, proprietary dataset (like company support transcripts) to specialize its output without the $10M cost of training from scratch.
To automatically debug the Python application utilizing the API.
To translate the LLM out of English into binary machine code.

questions.question: Why is 'Full Parameter Fine-Tuning' generally completely inaccessible for an average solo developer or small startup utilizing a modern, powerful LLM (e.g., 70 Billion weights)?

options.text: Because it mathematically requires loading the entire model and associated gradient states into GPU memory simultaneously, demanding thousands of dollars of enterprise A100 hardware hardware.
Because it requires explicit permission from the United States government.
Because Python doesn't support integers larger than 1 Billion natively.
Because open-source licenses strictly forbid it.

questions.question: What specific engineering breakthrough does the 'LoRA' (Low-Rank Adaptation) PEFT technique bring to model training?

options.text: It completely freezes the massive, original neural network and injects tiny, highly compressed mathematical matrices (adapters) into the transformer, calculating expensive gradient updates exclusively for the tiny matrices.
It automatically mines cryptocurrency simultaneously.
It skips the training step by brute-forcing a SQL database.
It executes purely using the CPU instead of the GPU.

questions.question: How does 'Quantization' function in the context of running massive AI models on consumer-grade hardware?

options.text: It splits the model into chunks and stores them sequentially on the hard drive.

It mathematically compresses the multi-dimensional weights of the model by reducing their precision (e.g., shrinking 16-bit floats down to 4-bit integers), drastically lowering the VRAM required to load the model.
It compresses the API responses using standard gzip format.
It removes random words from the training dataset to save space.

questions.question: What does the combination technique 'QLoRA' allow engineers to do computationally?

options.text: Query an LLM rapidly.

It combines massive Quantization (loading the base model in 4-bit) with LoRA training, shrinking the computational hardware requirements so dramatically that cutting-edge AI can be fine-tuned directly on a standard 24GB gaming GPU locally.
It enables LLMs to generate high-resolution images.
It guarantees perfect model accuracy.
